perturbation.original_text: The term “ Evaluation Material ” means information concerning the Provider which is furnished on or after November 8, 2011 to the Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below) on the Recipient's behalf in connection with the Recipient’s evaluation of a Possible Transaction, including its business, financial condition, operations, assets and liabilities, and includes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives to the extent containing or which are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished by the Provider hereunder, The term Evaluation Material does not include information which (i) is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives in breach of this Agreement, (ii) was within the Recipient’s or its Representatives possession prior to its being furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Provider, provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, (iii) is or becomes available to the Recipient or its Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Provider or its Representatives, provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, or (iv) was or is independently developed by the Recipient or the Recipient’s Representatives without the use of or reference to any Evaluation Material provided by or on behalf of Provider or its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “ Evaluation Material ” means information concerning the Provider which is furnished on or after November 8, 2011 to the Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below) on the Recipient's behalf in connection with the Recipient’s evaluation of a Possible Transaction, including its business, financial condition, operations, assets and liabilities, and includes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives to the extent containing or which are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished by the Provider hereunder. The term Evaluation Material *may* exclude information which (i) is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives in breach of this Agreement, (ii) was within the Recipient’s or its Representatives possession prior to its being furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Provider, provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, (iii) is or becomes available to the Recipient or its Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Provider or its Representatives, provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, or (iv) was or is independently developed by the Recipient or the Recipient’s Representatives without the use of or reference to any Evaluation Material provided by or on behalf of Provider or its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'does not include' to '*may* exclude', the definition of Evaluation Material becomes ambiguous. Previously, items (i) through (iv) were explicitly excluded from the definition. Now, their exclusion is discretionary, creating an in-text contradiction about what constitutes Evaluation Material. This directly contradicts the original unambiguous definition provided in section 2(a), as now some information that would not be Evaluation Material can now be considered as such and the term Evaluation Material, is now ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definitions . (a) The term “ Evaluation Material ” means information concerning the Provider which is furnished on or after November 8, 2011 to the Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below) on the Recipient's behalf in connection with the Recipient’s evaluation of a Possible Transaction, including its business, financial condition, operations, assets and liabilities, and includes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives to the extent containing or which are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished by the Provider hereunder. The term Evaluation Material *may* exclude information which (i) is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives in breach of this Agreement, (ii) was within the Recipient’s or its Representatives possession prior to its being furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Provider, provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, (iii) is or becomes available to the Recipient or its Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Provider or its Representatives, provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, or (iv) was or is independently developed by the Recipient or the Recipient’s Representatives without the use of or reference to any Evaluation Material provided by or on behalf of Provider or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Evaluation Material' by changing 'does not include' to '*may* exclude' for certain types of information. This introduces ambiguity, as the information previously excluded from the definition is now potentially included, which contradicts the original unambiguous definition. The contradicted text, which is actually the same text as the changed text (i.e. the location of the original text), is now internally inconsistent due to this change, as it offers conflicting possibilities for what constitutes 'Evaluation Material', making the meaning unclear and creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction. A reasonable reader would recognize the sections as incompatible because the change directly affects the clarity of the term 'Evaluation Material'.

perturbation.original_text: The term “ Representatives ” in the case of JDA shall mean the directors, officers, employees, counsel, investment bankers, financial advisors, agents, consultants, advisors, accountants or auditors of JDA. The term “ Representatives ” in the case of RHI shall mean the directors, officers, employees, counsel, and with prior written consent of JDA (not to be unreasonably withheld), investment bankers, financial advisors, potential sources of capital or financing (debt or equity), agents, consultants, advisors, accountants or auditors of RHI. Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing in this Agreement shall restrict the ability of RHI to discuss with, or engage, Greenhill & Co, or Bain & Company to act as its consultant, investment banker or financial advisor in connection with a Possible Transaction or to discuss with, or engage, Deloitte as its advisor or accountant in connection with a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “ Representatives ” in the case of JDA shall mean the directors, officers, employees, counsel, investment bankers, financial advisors, agents, consultants, advisors, accountants or auditors of JDA. The term “ Representatives ” in the case of RHI shall mean the directors, officers, employees, counsel, investment bankers, financial advisors, potential sources of capital or financing (debt or equity), agents, consultants, advisors, accountants or auditors of RHI. Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing in this Agreement shall restrict the ability of RHI to discuss with, or engage, Greenhill & Co, or Bain & Company to act as its consultant, investment banker or financial advisor in connection with a Possible Transaction or to discuss with, or engage, Deloitte as its advisor or accountant in connection with a Possible Transaction. *However, any engagement with Bain & Company requires pre-approval from JDA.*
perturbation.explanation: By adding the sentence '*However, any engagement with Bain & Company requires pre-approval from JDA.*', an in-text contradiction is introduced. The original definition stated that 'nothing in this Agreement shall restrict the ability of RHI to discuss with, or engage, Greenhill & Co, or Bain & Company'. This new clause now restricts RHI's ability to engage Bain & Company without JDA's pre-approval, which directly contradicts the original statement in section 2(b).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definitions . (b) The term “ Representatives ” in the case of JDA shall mean the directors, officers, employees, counsel, investment bankers, financial advisors, agents, consultants, advisors, accountants or auditors of JDA. The term “ Representatives ” in the case of RHI shall mean the directors, officers, employees, counsel, and with prior written consent of JDA (not to be unreasonably withheld), investment bankers, financial advisors, potential sources of capital or financing (debt or equity), agents, consultants, advisors, accountants or auditors of RHI. Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing in this Agreement shall restrict the ability of RHI to discuss with, or engage, Greenhill & Co, or Bain & Company to act as its consultant, investment banker or financial advisor in connection with a Possible Transaction or to discuss with, or engage, Deloitte as its advisor or accountant in connection with a Possible Transaction. *However, any engagement with Bain & Company requires pre-approval from JDA.*
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds '*However, any engagement with Bain & Company requires pre-approval from JDA.*' to the paragraph. This directly contradicts the original statement 'nothing in this Agreement shall restrict the ability of RHI to discuss with, or engage, Greenhill & Co, or Bain & Company', which is present in both the changed text and the contradicted text. This new clause now restricts RHI's ability to engage Bain & Company without JDA's pre-approval, which directly contradicts the original statement.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient and its Representatives will use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and, subject to Section 5, will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that any of such information may be disclosed to the Recipient’s Representatives for the purpose of helping the Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction. The Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of the Recipient’s Representatives, other than those of Recipient’s unaffiliated Representatives who have entered into a separate confidentiality agreement with the Provider. This Agreement does not grant the Recipient or any of its Representatives any license to use the Provider’s Evaluation Material except as provided herein. For the avoidance of doubt, RHI agrees that it shall not disclose any of the Evaluation Material provided by or on behalf of JDA to any of its affiliates that are not included in the definition of RHI.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient and its Representatives will use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and, subject to Section 5, will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that any of such information may be disclosed to the Recipient’s Representatives for the purpose of helping the Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction. The Recipient *is generally not* responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of the Recipient’s Representatives, other than those of Recipient’s unaffiliated Representatives who have entered into a separate confidentiality agreement with the Provider. This Agreement does not grant the Recipient or any of its Representatives any license to use the Provider’s Evaluation Material except as provided herein. For the avoidance of doubt, RHI agrees that it shall not disclose any of the Evaluation Material provided by or on behalf of JDA to any of its affiliates that are not included in the definition of RHI.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'agrees to be responsible' to '*is generally not* responsible', the level of responsibility becomes ambiguous. This creates a direct in-text contradiction in section 3 regarding the extent to which the Recipient is liable for its Representatives' actions. It introduces uncertainty about whether a breach by a Representative will be attributable to the Recipient, which directly affects contract enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Evaluation Material . The Recipient and its Representatives will use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and, subject to Section 5, will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that any of such information may be disclosed to the Recipient’s Representatives for the purpose of helping the Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction. The Recipient *is generally not* responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of the Recipient’s Representatives, other than those of Recipient’s unaffiliated Representatives who have entered into a separate confidentiality agreement with the Provider. This Agreement does not grant the Recipient or any of its Representatives any license to use the Provider’s Evaluation Material except as provided herein. For the avoidance of doubt, RHI agrees that it shall not disclose any of the Evaluation Material provided by or on behalf of JDA to any of its affiliates that are not included in the definition of RHI.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that 'The Recipient agrees to be responsible'. The changed text modifies this to 'The Recipient is generally not responsible'. This creates a direct conflict with the Contradicted Text, which contains the changed text, regarding the Recipient's liability for breaches by its Representatives, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction, as it introduces uncertainty about the extent to which the Recipient is liable, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: If the Recipient or its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, other requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material or any of the facts disclosure of which is prohibited 2 under Section 4 above, the Recipient or such Representative shall provide the Provider with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement. If, in the absence of a protective order or other remedy or the receipt of a waiver by the Provider, the Recipient or any of its Representatives is nonetheless legally compelled or required by law to disclose Evaluation Material or any of the facts disclosure of which is prohibited under Section 4, the Recipient or its Representatives may, without liability hereunder, disclose to such requiring Person only that portion of such Evaluation Material or any such facts which the Recipient or its Representatives is legally compelled or required by law to disclose; provided that the Recipient and/or its Representatives exercise its commercially reasonable efforts, at the Provider’s sole expense, to preserve the confidentiality of such Evaluation Material or any of such facts, including, without limitation, by reasonably cooperating with the Provider to obtain an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Evaluation Material or such facts by the Person receiving the material.
perturbation.changed_text: If the Recipient or its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, other requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material or any of the facts disclosure of which is prohibited 2 under Section 4 above, the Recipient or such Representative *may* provide the Provider with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement. If, in the absence of a protective order or other remedy or the receipt of a waiver by the Provider, the Recipient or any of its Representatives is nonetheless legally compelled or required by law to disclose Evaluation Material or any of the facts disclosure of which is prohibited under Section 4, the Recipient or its Representatives may, without liability hereunder, disclose to such requiring Person only that portion of such Evaluation Material or any such facts which the Recipient or its Representatives is legally compelled or required by law to disclose; provided that the Recipient and/or its Representatives exercise its commercially reasonable efforts, at the Provider’s sole expense, to preserve the confidentiality of such Evaluation Material or any of such facts, including, without limitation, by reasonably cooperating with the Provider to obtain an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Evaluation Material or such facts by the Person receiving the material.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall provide' to '*may* provide' makes the notification process optional. The original text placed a clear obligation on the Recipient to inform the Provider, but the change now allows the Recipient to choose whether or not to provide notice. This creates an in-text contradiction within section 5 regarding the mandatory nature of notification, potentially affecting the Provider's ability to protect its confidential information. The original obligation of the Recipient is now contradicted with the possibility of them not notifying the provider.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Legally Required Disclosure . If the Recipient or its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, other requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material or any of the facts disclosure of which is prohibited 2 under Section 4 above, the Recipient or such Representative *may* provide the Provider with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement. If, in the absence of a protective order or other remedy or the receipt of a waiver by the Provider, the Recipient or any of its Representatives is nonetheless legally compelled or required by law to disclose Evaluation Material or any of the facts disclosure of which is prohibited under Section 4, the Recipient or its Representatives may, without liability hereunder, disclose to such requiring Person only that portion of such Evaluation Material or any such facts which the Recipient or its Representatives is legally compelled or required by law to disclose; provided that the Recipient and/or its Representatives exercise its commercially reasonable efforts, at the Provider’s sole expense, to preserve the confidentiality of such Evaluation Material or any of such facts, including, without limitation, by reasonably cooperating with the Provider to obtain an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Evaluation Material or such facts by the Person receiving the material.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Recipient 'shall provide' notice, creating an obligation. The changed text modifies this to 'may provide,' making the notice optional. This directly contradicts the implicit requirement in the original text and the continued expectation within the contradicted section, that notice will be given. The change introduces ambiguity where certainty previously existed regarding the notification process, creating a strong in-text contradiction under the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: In that case, or at any time upon the written request of the Provider for any reason, the Recipient will, and will direct its Representatives to, within five business days after receipt of such notice or request, destroy or return all Evaluation Material in any way relating to the Provider or its products, services, employees or other assets or liabilities, and no copy or extract thereof (including electronic copies) shall be retained (except that one copy may be maintained by outside legal counsel to the Recipient for archival purposes), and the term of this Agreement shall be extended by a like number of days for each day that the Recipient or any of its Representatives is in non-compliance of this Section 8. The Recipient shall provide to the Provider a certificate of compliance with the previous sentence. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: In that case, or at any time upon the written request of the Provider for any reason, the Recipient will, and will direct its Representatives to, within five business days after receipt of such notice or request, *endeavor to* destroy or return all Evaluation Material in any way relating to the Provider or its products, services, employees or other assets or liabilities, and no copy or extract thereof (including electronic copies) shall be retained (except that one copy may be maintained by outside legal counsel to the Recipient for archival purposes), and the term of this Agreement shall be extended by a like number of days for each day that the Recipient or any of its Representatives is in non-compliance of this Section 8. The Recipient shall provide to the Provider a certificate of compliance with the previous sentence. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: By inserting '*endeavor to*' before 'destroy or return', the obligation to destroy or return the Evaluation Material becomes less stringent. The original text imposed a mandatory requirement. The insertion weakens the obligation to only an effort instead of a certainty, creating an in-text contradiction within section 8 regarding the enforceability of the return/destruction requirement. It also makes the penalty clause based on non-compliance (extending the agreement's term) more ambiguous in its application, since what constitutes non-compliance is now uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination of Discussions . If either Party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction, it will promptly inform the other Party of that decision. In that case, or at any time upon the written request of the Provider for any reason, the Recipient will, and will direct its Representatives to, within five business days after receipt of such notice or request, *endeavor to* destroy or return all Evaluation Material in any way relating to the Provider or its products, services, employees or other assets or liabilities, and no copy or extract thereof (including electronic copies) shall be retained (except that one copy may be maintained by outside legal counsel to the Recipient for archival purposes), and the term of this Agreement shall be extended by a like number of days for each day that the Recipient or any of its Representatives is in non-compliance of this Section 8. The Recipient shall provide to the Provider a certificate of compliance with the previous sentence. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The original text obligates the recipient to 'destroy or return' evaluation material, creating a clear and mandatory action. The changed text inserts 'endeavor to' before this obligation, weakening it to a mere effort. The Contradicted Text and Changed Text are in the same location, the change from mandatory to suggestive creates an in-text contradiction by creating ambiguity with the enforceability of the return/destruction requirement. It also contradicts the penalty clause, which says 'the term of this Agreement shall be extended by a like number of days for each day that the Recipient or any of its Representatives is in non-compliance of this Section 8', thus meeting the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party understands and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Party or any of its Representatives and that the Party against which such breach is committed shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof without the requirement of posting a bond or other security. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by either Party of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Party against which such breach is committed. If a court of competent jurisdiction, pursuant to a final, non-appealable order, determines that the Recipient or any of its Representatives has breached this Agreement, the Recipient shall pay the reasonable costs (including legal fees and expenses) incurred by the Provider in enforcing this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party understands and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Party or any of its Representatives and that the Party against which such breach is committed shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof without the requirement of posting a bond or other security. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by either Party of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Party against which such breach is committed. *However, in cases where the breach is unintentional, money damages may be considered an adequate remedy.* If a court of competent jurisdiction, pursuant to a final, non-appealable order, determines that the Recipient or any of its Representatives has breached this Agreement, the Recipient shall pay the reasonable costs (including legal fees and expenses) incurred by the Provider in enforcing this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the sentence '*However, in cases where the breach is unintentional, money damages may be considered an adequate remedy.*', an in-text contradiction is introduced within section 16. The initial part of the paragraph asserts that money damages are insufficient, but the added sentence suggests they might be adequate in some cases (unintentional breach). This creates uncertainty regarding the appropriate remedy for a breach and contradicts the explicit statement that equitable relief is available without the need for a bond or security. What was clearly considered an insuficent remedy, now can potentially be an adequate remedy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies . Each Party understands and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Party or any of its Representatives and that the Party against which such breach is committed shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof without the requirement of posting a bond or other security. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by either Party of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Party against which such breach is committed. *However, in cases where the breach is unintentional, money damages may be considered an adequate remedy.* If a court of competent jurisdiction, pursuant to a final, non-appealable order, determines that the Recipient or any of its Representatives has breached this Agreement, the Recipient shall pay the reasonable costs (including legal fees and expenses) incurred by the Provider in enforcing this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states money damages are 'not a sufficient remedy,' implying equitable relief is always available. The added sentence, '*However, in cases where the breach is unintentional, money damages may be considered an adequate remedy.*,' directly contradicts this by suggesting money damages can sometimes be sufficient. This creates ambiguity and inconsistency in the Remedies section, specifically with the initial statement that money damages are insufficient, which now may not always be true. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
